note:
赛博朋克2077开发人员CD Projekt Red宣布已成为目标网络攻击的受害者＆＃34;据称暴露了其许多游戏的源代码。
 在开发人员周二早上在Twitter上分享的消息中，黑客声称他们窃取了Cyber​​punk 2077，Gwent和The Witcher 3（包括未发布的最后一个原型）的受严格保护的源代码。与会计，行政，法律，人力资源，投资者关系等相关的文件＆＃34;据称也受到了损害。
  虽然黑客显然使用勒索软件将CDPR锁定在其系统的某些部分之外，但该公司表示，它应该能够通过备份恢复对数据的访问。面对金额不明的赎金需求，该公司也表现出反抗的态度，称尽管有释放被盗数据的威胁，但它仍不会进行谈判。 ＆＃34;我们不会屈服于要求，也不会与参与者协商，因为意识到这样做最终可能会导致泄露被盗用的数据，＆＃34;该公司写道。需求的截止日期为48小时。 CDPR表示，就我们所知，受到破坏的系统不包含我们的播放器或服务用户的任何个人数据。这与最近针对Capcom的勒索软件攻击有所不同，在这种攻击中，Capcom商店的客户，员工和电子竞技团队成员可能已经向黑客泄露了他们的个人信息。
     通常将游戏的原始源代码（用于创建分发给玩家的可执行文件）视为开发人员最有价值的商业秘密之一。早在2003年，Valve当时未发布的Half-Life 2的源代码泄漏导致一名德国黑客被捕。最近，作为所谓的“ Gigaleak”的一部分，在线发布了许多经典任天堂游戏的源代码。网络安全分析公司Coveware的最新报告发现，随着越来越多的公司拒绝付款，勒索软件攻击的总支出在经过数年稳步增长之后，在2020年第四季度略有下降。 Coveware发现，如今越来越多的这类攻击包括威胁在线数据泄漏，即使支付了所需的赎金，黑客也经常释放这些数据。
  回答您的标题：约翰尼·西尔弗汉德（Johnny Silverhand）会为黑客服务-直到他们认为金钱比关闭公司更重要为止。 ＆＃39;勒索＆＃39;不会成为他随身携带的东西。
note_en:
Cyberpunk 2077 developer CD Projekt Red announced that it has "become the victim of a targeted cyberattack" that allegedly exposed the source code for many of its games.
 In  a message the developer shared on Twitter Tuesday morning, the hackers allege that they stole the closely guarded source code for  Cyberpunk 2077,  Gwent, and  The Witcher 3 (including an unreleased prototype of the last). Documents "relating to accounting, administration, legal, HR, investors relations and more" were also allegedly compromised.
  While the hackers apparently used ransomware software to lock CDPR out of certain parts of its systems, the company says it should be able to restore access to the data through backups. The company also remained defiant in the face of a ransom demand of an unspecified amount, saying it wouldn't negotiate, despite a threat to release the stolen data. "We will not give in to the demands nor negotiate with the actor, being aware that this may eventually lead to the release of the compromised data," the company wrote. The demand came with a 48-hour deadline. CDPR says that "to the best of our knowledge, the compromised systems did not contain any personal data of our players or users of our services." That sets this attack apart from  a recent ransomware attack against Capcom, in which Capcom store customers, employees, and esports team members were among the groups that may have had their personal information revealed to hackers.
     The raw source code for a game, which is used to create the executable files distributed to players, is  usually considered to be among a developer's most valuable trade secrets. Back in 2003, the  leak of source code for Valve's then-unreleased  Half-Life 2 led to the arrest of a German hacker. More recently,  a vast tranche of source code for classic Nintendo games was released online as part of a so-called "Gigaleak." A  recent report by cybersecurity analysis firm Coveware found that total ransomware attack payments dipped slightly in the fourth quarter of 2020, after rising steadily for years prior, as more companies refuse to pay. An increasing number of those attacks now include threats to leak data online, Coveware found, and hackers often release that data even after the desired ransom is paid.
  In answer to your caption:  Johnny Silverhand would be working for the hackers - right up until they decided money was more important than taking down the corp. 'Ransom' wouldn't be something he'd be on-board with.
title: 赛博朋克2077开发人员遭受勒索软件攻击
title_en: Cyberpunk 2077 developer hit with ransomware attack

note:
大约两年前，当时的美国国家航空航天局局长吉姆·布莱登斯汀（Jim Bridenstine）在参议院委员会讨论美国在太空的领导权问题上出人意料。他感受到了按时完成任务的压力-考虑到其项目的范围和复杂性，NASA很难做到这一点。到2020年6月，一艘无人猎户座飞船向月球发射时，布莱登斯汀的思想仍处于正轨。
 ＆＃34;我认为，作为代理机构，我们必须恪守承诺，＆＃34;他在委员会会议上告诉了少数参议员。 ＆＃34;如果我们告诉您和其他人，我们将在2020年6月绕月球发射，我认为我们应该在2020年6月绕月球发射。然后，在谈到商业航天业时，他补充说，＆＃34;我们现在拥有惊人的功能，我们可以使用现成的功能来实现此目标。＆＃34;
  这听起来可能不太戏剧性，但是在太空政策和国会听证会上，这是异端。国会在2011年制造了“太空发射系统”火箭，并将其强行送给了一个不情愿的白宫。现在，他们被告知，航天局实际上并不需要大型火箭来执行其最初的任务。几天后，当布恩登斯汀建议SpaceX的“猎鹰”重型火箭可以将人类推向月球时，他又把这个异端推向了更远。在所有这些之后不久，布莱恩丁汀就被要求与阿拉巴马州高级参议员理查德·谢尔比举行会议，理查德·谢尔比是强有力的参议院拨款委员会主席。实际上，八十岁的谢尔比控制着NASA的预算。此外，SLS火箭在他所在的马歇尔太空飞行中心进行管理。该计划提供了数千个工作机会。谢尔比很生气。在南部画架中，他告诉布莱登斯汀他应该辞职。
    NASA的管理人员是由白宫任命的，在这场与谢尔比（Shelby）的对决中，布莱登斯汀最终得到了副总统迈克·彭斯（Mike Pence）的支持。但是在任职的将近两年时间里，布莱恩汀汀再也没有谈论过用SLS火箭以外的任何东西发射猎户座飞船。几周后，在谢尔比拨款委员会举行的听证会上，布莱登斯汀表现得特别尊敬。他说，只有SLS火箭才能满足NASA的需求。
 谢尔比（Shelby）在美国参议院积累权力和资历后的十年中，对美国国家航空航天局（NASA）的人类航天计划施加了这种权力。但是现在，在民主党控制参议院之后，他处于少数派。本周，谢尔比宣布他不会在2022年竞选连任。那么，这对NASA和太空政策意味着什么呢？
  谢尔比（Shelby）具有过度劳累的言辞，并保护为他的竞选活动做出贡献并在阿拉巴马州开展业务的承包商。十多年前，他开始发挥自己的力量。
 2009年，奥巴马政府召集了一个由Norm Augustine领导的蓝丝带小组，以考虑NASA的人类太空飞行未来。他们在2010年初作出了判决：他们的计划是不可持续的，其中包括制造大型火箭的计划。这导致奥巴马政府取消了在阿拉巴马州管理的这枚Ares V火箭。 
谢尔比成为保护马歇尔太空飞行中心的重要堡垒。毕竟，这是土星五号和航天飞机助推器设计的地方。美国国家航空航天局（NASA）一直以来都会通过马歇尔太空飞行中心（Marshall Space Flight Center）探索深空的能力，他当时说。
 当奥巴马政府进一步建议美国宇航局（NASA）转向更多地依靠商业发射业时，阿拉巴马州参议员加强了针对奥巴马和美国宇航局副局长洛里·加弗（Lori Garver）的言论。他说：“总统提议的NASA预算开始为美国人类太空飞行的未来走向死亡。”国会不能也不会坐下来观看鲁sound放弃合理原则，可靠的往绩记录，迈向成功的稳步道路以及对人类太空飞行计划的破坏。
    为了进行报复，谢尔比不仅帮助领导了参议院开发太空发射系统火箭以取代Ares V的努力，而且还积极反对资助商业船员计划使用私人火箭和航天器将宇航员运送到国际太空站。
 当然，十年后，尽管资金延迟多年，“商业船员”计划仍开始交付。同时，SLS计划继续遇到问题，并且在花费200亿美元的NASA资金后，仍然可能至少有一年要通过车辆的试飞。这是原本计划在2016年发射的火箭。谢尔比（Shelby）无疑是为他的选民们交付的，但他却妨碍了航天局。
  加弗（Garver）于2013年9月离开美国宇航局（NASA），她说她相信谢尔比（Shelby）的离开可以为与国会达成更多有关太空政策的协议打开大门。
 参议员谢尔比（Shelby Shelby）对位于其阿拉巴马州的美国国家航空航天局（NASA）计划的大力倡导，无可估量地影响了人类的太空飞行，这导致拨款者反对商业船员的资金，而赞成增加SLS，＆＃34;她告诉Ars。 ＆＃34;作为与商业船员作战并要求NASA建造自己的火箭的四位参议员中的最后一位，他的离任可能会为政府与向前迈进的希尔之间的合作开辟新的领域。
  在过去十年中，世界发生了很大变化。波音等大型承包商已经在为NASA建造SLS，而新的太空竞争对手如SpaceX和Blue Origin出现了具有成本竞争力的大型火箭。 SpaceX已经展示了自己的私人开发的Falcon Heavy助推器，并开始发布其下一代Starship飞行器的原型。 
如果谢尔顿（Shelby）离开，它将继续使Biden白宫更容易取消SLS火箭计划，前提是该计划继续面临技术难题，例如核心阶段的热火测试失败。 如果SpaceX成功将Starship的Super Heavy火箭送入轨道，这也将使该计划的结束更加不可避免。 如果没有像谢尔比这样的强大后盾，重型火箭的实际成本要比SLS低得多，并且具有更大的升力，并且能够多次重复使用，这是不容忽视的。
note_en:
Nearly two years ago then-NASA administrator Jim Bridenstine  made a remarkable appearance before a Senate committee to discuss US leadership in space. He was feeling the pressure to keep deadlines—something NASA struggles to do given the scope and complexity of its projects. At the top of Bridenstine's mind was remaining on track for a June 2020 launch of an uncrewed Orion spacecraft to the Moon.
 "I think we as an agency need to stick to our commitment," he told a handful of senators in the committee meeting. "If we tell you, and others, that we’re going to launch in June of 2020 around the Moon, I think we should launch around the Moon in June of 2020." Then, referencing the commercial space industry, he added, "We have amazing capability that exists right now that we can use off-the-shelf in order to accomplish this objective."
  This may not sound too dramatic, but in the realm of space policy and Congressional hearings, this was  heresy. Congress had created the Space Launch System rocket in 2011, and forced it upon an unwilling White House. Now, they were being told the space agency did not actually need the large rocket to fly the very missions it was created for. Days later, Bridenstine took this heresy further  when he suggested SpaceX's Falcon Heavy rocket could boost humans to the Moon. Shortly after all of this, Bridenstine was called to a meeting with Richard Shelby, the senior senator from Alabama who chaired the powerful Senate Appropriations committee. Effectively, the octogenarian Shelby controlled NASA's budget. Moreover, the SLS rocket was being managed in his state, at the Marshall Space Flight Center. The program was worth thousands of jobs. Shelby was livid. In his southern drawl, he told Bridenstine he should resign.
    NASA administrators are appointed by the White House, and Bridenstine ultimately received backing from Vice President Mike Pence in this showdown with Shelby. But never again during the nearly two years left in his tenure did Bridenstine talk about launching the Orion spacecraft on anything but an SLS rocket. Weeks later, at a hearing before Shelby's appropriations committee,  Bridenstine was especially deferential. Only the SLS rocket, he said, could meet NASA's needs.
 Shelby wielded this kind of power over NASA's human spaceflight program for the better part of a decade as he accumulated power and seniority in the US Senate. But now, he is in the minority after Democrats took control of the Senate. And this week, Shelby announced that he would not run for re-election in 2022. So what does this all mean for NASA and space policy?
  Shelby has a legacy of overwrought rhetoric as well as protecting contractors that contributed to his campaign and did business in Alabama. He began flexing his power more than a decade ago.
 In 2009, the Obama administration convened a blue-ribbon panel, led by Norm Augustine, to consider NASA's human spaceflight future. In early 2010 they returned with their verdict: The agency's plan, which included building a very large rocket, was unsustainable. This led the Obama administration to cancel this Ares V rocket, which was managed in Alabama.
  Shelby emerged as the key bulwark to protect Marshall Space Flight Center. After all, this was where the Saturn V and space shuttle boosters were designed. The ability of NASA to explore deep space "has always been and always will be through Marshall Space Flight Center,"  he said at the time.
 When the Obama administration further suggested that NASA should pivot to relying more on the commercial launch industry, the Alabama senator stepped up his rhetoric aimed at Obama and NASA's deputy administrator, Lori Garver. “The president’s proposed NASA budget begins the death march for the future of US human space flight,”  he said. "Congress cannot and will not sit back and watch the reckless abandonment of sound principles, a proven track record, a steady path to success, and the destruction of our human space flight program."
    In retaliation, Shelby not only helped lead the Senate's effort to develop the Space Launch System rocket to replace the Ares V, but also actively opposed funding the Commercial Crew program to use private rockets and spacecraft to carry astronauts to the International Space Station.
 A decade later, of course, the Commercial Crew program has begun to deliver despite years of funding delays. Meanwhile, the SLS Program continues to suffer problems, and after spending $20 billion NASA is still likely at least a year from a test flight for the vehicle. This, for a rocket originally supposed to launch in 2016. While Shelby undoubtedly delivered for his constituents,  he hamstrung the space agency.
  Garver, who left NASA in September 2013, said she believed that Shelby's departure could open the door to more deal-making on space policy with Congress.
 "Senator Shelby’s strong advocacy for NASA programs based in his home state of Alabama has shaped human space flight immeasurably—leading the appropriators to oppose Commercial Crew funding in favor of increases for SLS," she told Ars. "As the last of the four Senators who fought against Commercial Crew and demanded NASA build its own rocket, his departure could open up new areas for cooperation between the administration and the Hill moving forward."
  The world has changed a lot over the last decade. While large contractors such as Boeing have been building the SLS for NASA, new space competitors such as SpaceX and Blue Origin have emerged with cost-competitive large rockets. SpaceX has already demonstrated its own, privately developed Falcon Heavy booster, and it is beginning to launch prototypes of its next-generation Starship vehicle.
 It seems probable that Shelby's departure would make it easier for the Biden White House to cancel the SLS rocket program should it continue to face technical difficulties, such as the  failed hot-fire test of the core stage. It will also make the program's end all the more inevitable should SpaceX succeed in launching Starship into orbit on its Super Heavy rocket. Without a potent backstop like Shelby, the reality of a heavy-lift rocket that costs significantly less than SLS, has a greater lift capacity, and is capable of multiple reuses should be impossible to ignore.
title: 这么久的谢尔比参议员：SLS火箭的主要架构师不会寻求连任
title_en: So long Senator Shelby: Key architect of SLS rocket won’t seek reelection

note:
这位高管说：“我欢迎打这样的电话，信息是关于共同发展和共同成功的。”他指出，愿意与新政府进行对话。 “美国希望经济增长，中国也希望经济增长。”
 华为在美国的未来一直是新政府面临的一个重大问号。在特朗普执政期间，许多具有影响力的中国公司被添加到商务部的所谓“实体清单”中，从而产生各种影响。华为一直是受此举影响最大的公司之一。
   除了阻止在全球第三大智能手机市场上的销售外，该公司还无法与包括谷歌在内的美国主要公司合作。反过来，这又阻碍了对关键技术（包括Android生态系统）的访问，并让华为争先恐后。该公司在中国大陆消费者的支持有所增加，但此举对智能手机制造商的底线造成了重大打击。
 即将上任的拜登政府在此问题上一直保持沉默。尽管面对共和党议员越来越多的批评，商务部长提名人吉娜·雷蒙多（Gina Raimondo）此后补充说：“我目前没有理由认为那些名单上的实体不应该存在。如果得到确认，我期待着对这些实体和其他有关实体进行简报。”
 尽管到目前为止，对于华为并没有很多积极的迹象，但可以理解的是，该公司的负责人宁愿与新政府合作。
 任志刚在翻译后的评论中说：“如果能够扩大华为的生产能力，那将意味着美国公司也有更多的供应机会。” “我相信这将是互惠互利的。我相信，新政府将牢记将要决定其新政策的商业利益。”
note_en:
“I would welcome such phone calls and the message is around joint development and shared success,” the executive said, noting a readiness to speak with the new administration in translated remarks. “The U.S. wants to have economic growth and China wants to have economic growth as well.”
 Huawei’s future in the U.S. has been a major question mark hanging over the new administration. Under Trump, a number of high profile Chinese companies were added to the Commerce Department’s so-called “entity list” to various effects. Huawei has been among the hardest hit by the moves.
   In addition to blocking sales in the world’s third-largest smartphone market, the company has been unable to work with key U.S. companies, including Google. That, in turn, has blocked access to key technologies, including the Android ecosystem and left Huawei scrambling. The company’s support among consumers has increased within China, but the move has been a big blow to the smartphone maker’s bottom line.
 The incoming Biden administration has mostly been quiet on the matter. Though, facing mounting criticism from Republican lawmaker, Commerce Secretary nominee Gina Raimondo has since added that, “I currently have no reason to believe that entities on those lists should not be there. If confirmed, I look forward to a briefing on these entities and others of concern.”
 While there haven’t been many positive signs for Huawei thus far, the company’s Chief understandably would prefer to make nice with the new administration.
 “If Huawei’s production capacity can be expanded, that would mean more opportunities for U.S. companies to supply too,” Ren said in the translated comments. “I believe that’s going to be mutually beneficially. I believe that (the) new administration would bear in mind such business interests as they are about to decide their new policy.”
title: 华为首席执行官想与乔·拜登对话
title_en: Huawei’s CEO wants to talk to Joe Biden

note:
Aurora 7笔记本电脑似乎直接摆脱了好莱坞一名道具制作人员对黑客轻弹的研究。但是有了七个折叠式屏幕，几乎没有任何人可以真正在腿上使用这种野兽。这是一个移动转换工作站，适合那些需要更多屏幕空间而不是可以容纳显示器的人。
    Aurora 7由英国一家名为Expanscape的公司创建，在游戏的现阶段几乎只是一个原型（通过广泛使用3D打印部件可以明显看出这一点），但它被设计为真正的移动工作站，适合开发人员使用内容创作者，甚至是资金充裕的游戏玩家，都希望从计算机上获得身临其境的体验，而不必离开家。
   Aurora 7由支持64GB DDR4 RAM的Intel i9 9900K处理器和NVIDIA GTX 1060系列显卡提供支持，还配备2TB硬盘驱动器和额外的2.5TB SSD存储，以及您可能需要的所有端口。进一步扩大其能力。但是，展览的明星是复杂的屏幕拼接，包括四个17.3英寸4K（3840 x 2160）LCD（两个处于纵向模式，两个处于横向），以及三个较小的7英寸屏幕，均达到1920 x 1200像素，其中一个位于笔记本电脑的腕托中。
   单击此处，让您的心灵吹牛到本周结束，即可获得振动棒，戒指和尖端性技术的折扣。
 可能更令人印象深刻的是，所有这些屏幕均经过精心设计，可以折叠起来，形成一个可以放在手提袋中的扁平外形，尽管这个手提袋足够大，可以存放一台4.3英寸厚的笔记本电脑，重达26英寸。磅。一切完成后，Aurora 7的创造者希望将其重量减少到22磅，但这不是您想每天拖着办公室回到办公室的笔记本电脑。这是您要为其构建自定义轮式推车的机器。
  尽管Aurora 7目前仅以原型形式存在，但Expanscape仍将其产品出售给要求比以前用在笔记本电脑上的鞋拔更多的像素的消费者。但是，该公司不仅不公开定价信息，还要求感兴趣的买家签署保密协议，承诺就自己为同类移动工作站实际支付的费用保持沉默。通常，由于要花费大量时间和金钱来制作单个零件，原型总是要比面向消费者的小工具价格更高。在生产线上成千上万个它们可以大大降低成本，但是如果Aurora 7可以向公众提供价格接近合理的价格，那就不要抱太大希望。
note_en:
The   Aurora 7 laptop seems lifted straight from the imagination of a Hollywood prop builder working on a bad hacker flick. But with seven foldout screens, there’s little chance anyone could actually use this beast on their laps. It’s a mobile transforming workstation for those who need more screen real estate than they have room for monitors.
    Created by a UK company called   Expanscape, the Aurora 7 is very much just a prototype at this stage in the game (as is evident by the extensive use of 3D-printed parts), but it’s designed to be true mobile workstation for everyone from developers to content creators to even well-funded gamers wanting a more immersive experience from a computer they don’t have to leave at home.
   Powered by an Intel i9 9900K processor backed by 64GB of DDR4 RAM and an NVIDIA GTX 1060 series graphics card, the Aurora 7 also comes with 2TB of hard drive storage and an additional 2.5 TB of SSD storage, plus all the ports you could ever need to expand its capacity even further. But the star of the show is the complicated mosaic of screens which includes four 17.3-inch 4K (3840 x 2160) LCDs—two in portrait mode and two in landscape—as well as three smaller 7-inch screens all pushing 1920 x 1200 pixels, with one located in the laptop’s wrist rest.
   Click Here To Have Your Mind Blown  Get discounts on vibrators, rings, and cutting-edge sex tech now through the end of the week.
 Possibly even more impressive is that all those screens are engineered to fold up onto themselves to create a flat profile that can be carried in a bag—albeit a bag large enough to stash a 4.3-inch-thick laptop that weighs in at a hefty 26 pounds. The Aurora 7's creators are hoping to trim its weight to a lean 22 pounds when all is said and done, but this is not a laptop you want to haul to the office and back every day. This is a machine you’ll want to build a custom wheeled cart for.
  Although the Aurora 7 only exists in prototype form right now, Expanscape is still   offering to sell its creations to consumers demanding more pixels than have ever been shoe-horned onto a laptop before. But not only is the company not making pricing info public, it’s also requiring interested buyers to sign an NDA promising to keep mum on how much money they actually paid for their one-of-a-kind mobile workstation. In general, prototypes always cost more than a consumer-ready version of a gadget given the time and money that has to go into creating individual parts. Making them by the thousands on a production line significantly reduces costs, but don’t get your hopes up for the Aurora 7 being anything close to reasonably priced if and when it is made available to the public.
title: 推出具有7个屏幕的Aurora 7笔记本电脑

note:
布鲁塞尔（路透社）-欧洲反托拉斯负责人玛格丽特·韦斯特格（Margrethe Vestager）警告苹果公司，由于iPhone制造商的隐私变更已引起竞争对手Facebook Inc.的反竞争做法指控，因此对其平台上的所有应用程序应给予同等待遇。
  苹果将​​在春季要求iPhone用户同意，以跟踪其个性化广告的数据，这是为了保护用户的隐私，但这将限制应用程序从可用于定向广告的人手机中收集数据的能力。
 Facebook一直是批评最激烈的批评者之一，因为苹果的举动将使其收入损失很大一部分。
 Facebook在12月的博客文章中称其为反竞争行为，称苹果自己的个性化广告平台将不受新要求的影响，使用户可以选择是否选择接受第三方跟踪。
  Vestager表示，尽管该问题与隐私相关，但如果苹果改变公平竞争环境，它可能演变成反托拉斯问题。
 她在周一的采访中对路透社表示：“如果证明苹果没有以相同的方式对待自己的应用程序，那可能就是竞争。”
 Vestager说，到目前为止，她尚未收到有关Apple更改的任何投诉。 
她说，隐私行动也与她去年提出的遏制美国科技巨头力量并允许消费者对其个人数据有更多控制权的提议相似。  “拥有明确的退出选项是一件非常好的事情。 如果您看一下《数字服务法案》，《数字市场法案》，这些就是我们正在研究的一些解决方案。”  苹果没有立即对此事发表评论。 该公司表示，选择退出选项将适用于所有跟踪用户的开发人员-包括苹果公司本身-但由于其广告平台无法跟踪用户，因此该选项是有争议的。  苹果目前是欧盟两项调查的目标，一项针对其移动支付系统Apple Pay，另一项针对其App Store。
note_en:
BRUSSELS (Reuters) - Europe’s antitrust chief, Margrethe Vestager, has warned Apple Inc to give equal treatment to all apps on its platform amid the iPhone maker’s privacy changes that have drawn charges of anti-competitive practices from rival Facebook Inc.
  Apple will in the spring ask iPhone users for consent to track their data for personalized ads in what it says is a move to protect users’ privacy but which will limit apps’ ability to gather data from people’s phones that can be used for targeted advertising.
 Facebook has been among the most vocal of the critics which stand to lose a substantial part of their revenue from Apple’s move.
 Facebook in a December blog post called it anti-competitive behaviour, saying that Apple’s own personalized ad platform would be exempt from the new requirement giving users a choice of whether to opt in to tracking by third parties.
  Vestager said while the issue is privacy-related, it can morph into an antitrust issue if Apple tilts the level playing field.
 “It can be competition if it is shown that Apple is not treating its own apps in the same way,” she told Reuters in an interview on Monday.
 Vestager said so far she has not received any complaint about Apple’s changes.
  She said the privacy move is also similar to her proposal unveiled last year to curb the powers of U.S. tech giants and allow consumers to have more control of their personal data.
 “It is a very good thing to have a clear opt-out option. If you look at the Digital Services Act, the Digital Markets Act, these are some of the solutions we are looking at there.”
 Apple was not immediately available for comment. The company has said the opt-out option would apply to all developers who track users - including Apple itself - but because its advertising platform does not track users, the option is a moot point.
 Apple is currently the target of two EU investigations, one into its mobile payment system, Apple Pay, and the other into its App Store.
title: 欧盟的Vestager警告苹果在隐私纠纷中平等对待所有应用
title_en: EU's Vestager Warns Apple To Treat All Apps Equally Amid Privacy Dispute

note:
Shopify是企业的商务平台，正在将其结帐和付款处理系统Shop Pay引入某些Facebook平台。
  Shop Pay选项将首先在周二提供给Instagram用户，并将在接下来的几周内在社交媒体公司的小型企业平台Facebook Shops中推出。
  消费者将能够使用Shop Pay完成购买，扩展现有选项以使用PayPal Holdings PayPal或手动输入信用卡或借记卡信息。所有这些方法都是通过Facebook Pay付款系统提供的。
  Shoppay存储信用卡和送货信息以加快在线结帐速度，以前在Shopify客户的电子商务商店之外尚不可用。
    Facebook和Instagram一直在努力成为人们发现产品并轻松购买产品的场所-这是一个成长为社会贸易领域的一部分。
    研究公司eMarketer的一份报告显示，今年美国的社交商务支出预计将增长34.8％，达到360.9亿美元，其中包括在社交平台或直接点击广告或其他社交媒体内容上进行的购买。
  但根据去年8月进行的eMarketer调查，只有9％的消费者通过社交平台进行定期购物。根据6月eMarketer的另一项调查，只有18.7％的通过社交商务购买商品的消费者使用了可用的结帐功能，而有57.8％的人点击并直接在零售商的网站上购买了商品。社交媒体公司宁愿将用户保留在其平台上。 
Shop Pay旨在减少购买摩擦，使购买东西更容易，而无需为每个订单输入信用卡或运送信息。
  注册用户将在结帐时选择“商店付款”，输入与该帐户关联的电子邮件地址和电话号码，并收到一条代码以验证它是他们首次在Facebook商店或Instagram上使用该代码。 Shop总经理Carl Rivera说，Shop Pay还可以帮助用户通过Shop应用程序跟踪包裹，提供分期付款的选项，并承诺抵消每次交付所产生的碳排放。根据其网站，该公司保护“同等数量的树木”以抵消排放。
  Rivera先生说，Shopify不断努力改善Shop Pay体验的各个方面。该公司正在努力“为我们的碳排放创造更多透明度，向用户展示；加快结帐速度；他正在做一些细微的调整和改进，这些改进和改进正在缓慢地推动转换的发展。
  他补充说：“我们认为，拿些小东西，也许被低估了，将其转变为类似于神奇体验的东西，具有很多优点和价值。”
  行业专家说，为社交媒体用户提供更多无需点击即可轻松付款的方式，可能会鼓励冲动购买和更频繁地购买。
  广告控股公司阳狮集团首席商务策略官杰森·戈德伯格说，但是Shop Pay的知名度或差异性不足，无法迅速提高销售额。
  戈德伯格说：“ Shopify Pay具有一定的发展势头，并且正在成长，并且很聪明地受到支持，但不太可能显着增加Facebook上能够使用数字钱包付款的购物者的数量。” 
戈德伯格补充说：“消费者不太可能根据他们在某些第三方应用中跟踪购买情况的能力来选择要购买的商品以及购买方式。”  Shopify在最新的季度收益报告中表示，Shop Pay在全球拥有6000万用户。  PayPal还提供分期付款计划等功能，最近一个季度报告了3.77亿活跃帐户，新增了1600万新帐户。  eMarketer首席分析师安德鲁·利普斯曼（Andrew Lipsman）表示，随着时间的流逝，社交商务将逐渐成为一种更为普遍的消费者行为，它最适合在美容，时尚和家居装饰等类别的冲动性购买中使用。  “人们需要高度参与，他们必须具有非常强的品牌亲和力，他们必须具有很高的欲望，并且他们在很多情况下都必须几乎立刻想购买的冲动才能购买。 以便结帐。”利普斯曼先生说。
note_en:
Shopify  a commerce platform for businesses, is bringing its checkout and payment processing system, Shop Pay, to some  Facebook  platforms.
  The Shop Pay option will first be available to Instagram users on Tuesday and will roll out on  Facebook Shops, the social-media company’s platform for small businesses, in the next few weeks.
  Consumers will be able to use Shop Pay to complete purchases, expanding on existing options to use  PayPal Holdings  PayPal or manually enter credit or debit card information. All these methods are offered via the Facebook Pay payment system.
  Shop Pay, which stores credit card and shipping information to speed online checkout, hasn’t previously been available outside the e-commerce stores of Shopify clients.
    Facebook and Instagram have been working to become venues for people to discover products and easily buy them—part of a growing area known as  social commerce.
    Social commerce spending is expected to rise 34.8% in the U.S. this year to $36.09 billion, counting purchases made on a social platform or by clicking directly on an advertisement or other content in social media, according to a report from eMarketer, a research firm.
  But only 9% of consumers buying through social platforms did so on a regular basis, according to an eMarketer survey conducted last August. And only 18.7% of consumers who purchased items via social commerce used the available checkout features, while 57.8% clicked away and bought directly on a retailer’s website, according to a different eMarketer survey in June. Social-media companies would rather keep users on their platforms.
  Shop Pay is designed to reduce purchase friction and make it easier to buy something without entering credit card or shipping information for every order.
  Registered users would select  Shop Pay at checkout, enter the email address and phone number associated with the account, and receive a code to verify it is them the first time they use it on Facebook Shops or Instagram. Shop Pay also helps users track a package via the Shop app, offers the option to pay in installments and promises to offset the carbon emissions associated with each delivery, said Carl Rivera, general manager of Shop. The company protects “an equivalent number of trees” to offset emissions, according to its website.
  Shopify continually tries to improve each aspect of the Shop Pay experience, Mr. Rivera said. The company is striving to “create more transparency about how we’re doing carbon emissions, show that to the user; make the checkout even faster; work on tiny tweaks and improvements that are slowly driving the conversion up and up,” he said.
  “We believe there’s a lot of merit and a lot of value to taking something small and perhaps, underappreciated and turn it into something akin to a magical experience,” he added.
  Giving social-media users more ways to easily pay without having to click might encourage impulse purchases and more frequent buying, industry experts said.
  But Shop Pay isn’t well known enough or different enough to quickly boost sales, said Jason Goldberg, chief commerce strategy officer at  Publicis Groupe  the advertising holding company.
  “Shopify Pay has some momentum and it’s growing and it’s smart to support, but it’s unlikely to dramatically increase the pool of shoppers on Facebook that are able to pay with a digital wallet,” Mr. Goldberg said.
  “Consumers aren’t likely to pick what they want to buy and how they want to pay for it based on their ability to track those purchases in some third-party app,” Mr. Goldberg added.
    Shop Pay has 60 million global users, Shopify said in its latest quarterly earnings report.
  PayPal, which also offers features such as installment payment plans, reported 377 million active accounts, adding 16 million new ones, in the most recent quarter.
    While social commerce will become a more common consumer behavior over time, it works best for impulse purchases in categories such as beauty, fashion and home décor, said Andrew Lipsman, principal analyst at eMarketer.
  “People need to be highly engaged, they have to have a really strong brand affinity, they have to have a high level of desire, and they have to almost feel that impulse to want to purchase in the moment in a lot of cases in order for that checkout to happen,” Mr. Lipsman said.
title: Shopify即将将其结帐和付款处理服务Shop Pay带到今天的Instagram和未来几周的Facebook商店中
title_en: Shopify is bringing its checkout and payment processing service, Shop Pay, to Instagram today and to Facebook Shops in the next few weeks

note:
Hacker News最初由YC联合创始人Paul Graham制作，以演示Arc（一种他一直在使用的新编程语言）。他很快意识到，这可以帮助将他所支持的公司和其他想要加入的人们聚集在一起。在给定的工作日内，页面浏览量为160万，唯一访问者为20万，如今，这已成为风险投资公司成功的关键部分。
 但是该网站很快就起飞了，因为以前的Redditor蜂拥而至，谈论技术和创业公司（该网站后来被称为Startup News）。
  正如格雷厄姆所解释的那样，当该站点立即开始受到牵引力时，他意识到这不仅仅是测试Arc的一种方法。他想让Hacker News成为重现Reddit过去美好时光的地方，当时Reddit的大多数社区都是由黑客组成的。随着Reddit吸引了更多的流量，该站点的黑客焦点也随之演变。社区的用户群随着增长而被稀释，并且Hacker News是一些早期Reddit黑客的新家。
  Reddit以前非常重视与启动相关的链接，但这是因为Reddit的许多初始用户都以某种方式连接到了Y Combinator。既然Reddit越来越受欢迎，则顶部链接通常是图像，视频或政治新闻。
 Graham说，Hacker News的另一个目标是在这里建立创始人可以共享思想和交流的地方。秉承Y Combinator自己的孵化器的精神，Hacker News专注于成为技术社区中的企业家和创始人的社区：一个他们可以自由张贴的地方，Y Combinator也可以结识技术领域的潜在创始人和领导人。
 “从一开始，我们就拥有了一个真正的社区，如今，来自Reddit的一些核心难民群体仍然在Hacker News上占据重要地位，” Graham解释说。吸引许多黑客新闻的部分原因是其简单性和投票系统。在过去六年中，产品的用户界面，设计和配色方案一直保持相对稳定。
 该网站的第一批用户之一Thomas Ptacek解释说，他是Slashdot用户，然后是Reddit用户，并蜂拥至Hacker News（当时称为Startup News），因为它与技术和启动社区更相关。他发现与过去的论坛相比，《黑客新闻》是一个令人耳目一新的变化。 
Hacker News的工作方式如下：用户提交故事链接，并根据与Reddit类似的投票系统对故事进行排名。但是，Hacker News和Reddit之间的区别在于投票系统。虽然您可以对故事进行投票，但不能对故事进行投票（但可以举报故事）。根据Graham的说法，将有100名支持者将其故事放在该网站首页的顶部。如果您在网站上有足够的“因果报应”，则只能对评论投反对票，这是Hacker News另一个引人注目的要素。业力因数由用户提交的内容和评论的不赞成投票数减去不赞成投票数决定。
 在设计方面，Graham说他希望Hacker News看起来像您在终端窗口中的进程列表。该网站的外观和风格针对的是熟悉表格数据的黑客本身。
  Graham偶尔会添加新功能，其中一些功能位于网站的后端。例如，随着评论在交流中变得更加嵌套和激烈，回复链接需要更长的时间才能显示。格雷厄姆说，这是有目的的拖累，因为深入嵌套的讨论很少有趣。
 另一个微妙的功能是：火焰探测器。 Graham一直在不断部署和更新专有软件，该软件可以确定是否发生了人们激烈争论的火焰战争。当发生这些火焰大战时（格雷厄姆说这经常变得丑陋和个性化），进行评论的故事在页面的下方移动。
 Graham还创建了复杂的垃圾邮件检测软件，六个月前刚刚用新代码对其进行了更新。格雷厄姆表示，在更新后，垃圾邮件在网站上停留的时间通常不会超过10分钟。如果用户向该站点发送垃圾邮件或进行个人恶意行为，则有被禁止的风险。但是，在一种有趣的方式（称为“地狱禁止”）中，用户实际上可能并不知道自己被禁止了。
 在后端，Hacker News运行在一个核心上，Graham将其称为“显着的扩展壮举”。
 在人工审核方面，Graham本人每天仅花三到四个小时就对网站进行审核。这是他负责Y Combinator的所有职责的补充。尽管其他许多YC校友具有管理能力，但Graham一直是该网站的主要人为元素。他说：“这已经成为我的生活。”大约六个月前，格雷厄姆（Graham）聘请了他不愿透露姓名的其他人来管理该网站。他说，这个人隶属于Y Combinator，是个“审慎周到的人”，从那以后一直做得很好。 
Hacker News与Y Combinator也有很强的联系。 Graham解释说，创始人通常在申请时都会创建一个Hacker News帐户，并且用户名是Y Combinator的创始人身份。 Hacker News还设有一个工作页面，该页面显示Y Combinator公司提供的所有工作。他补充说，这个工作门户网站对于Y Combinator非常有用，因为该网站的大多数受众都是程序员和工程师。
 如果您是YC创始人，则您的用户名将以橙色显示给其他YC创始人，以使这些企业家能够相互认识并认识。
 格雷厄姆说，《黑客新闻》引起了很多抱怨，因为它偏向于介绍有关Y Combinator初创公司的故事，但他说没有这种偏见。取而代之的是，孵化器的文化是使用Hacker News，而已有1000多名YC校友从孵化器毕业，这些创始人中的许多人仍然活跃在新闻网站上，并发布指向其创始人的发布会和新闻的链接。
 “这是一个知识分子小村庄，现在是一个大城市。”
 增长有其不利之处。格雷厄姆彻夜难眠的原因是担心《黑客新闻》质量的下降。他解释说，该网站是黑客界内部人士的社区，并且已逐渐被稀释。他说：“这就是我一直在思考的事情。”
 他担心《黑客新闻》将成为他所谓的“一座破旧的老建筑”。
 他说：“由于网站的增长，社区一直处于永久但缓慢的衰退中。” 
Ptacek同意Hacker News的价值已经有所改变。他说：“社区让我感觉不那么好，而在一开始的时候，这是一小撮彼此认识的人。” “现在不太可能在线程之间看到相同的人。”
 格雷厄姆最大的痛点之一就是他每天在网站上发现的“校园争吵”，并希望“用户停止行为不端”。他举例说明了用户组织投票环以有目的地对故事进行投票的例子，这使Graham开发了其他软件来检测此情况。他补充说，越来越多的用户正在使用新创建的帐户进行拖钓，并有意在该网站上发动火焰大战。
 他说：“我希望我能使人们停止发表愚蠢或刻薄的评论。” “只需要一两个负面评论，一场讨论就变成了一场火焰大战。”
 Graham补充说，他个人因偏见或审查而从用户身上得到了很多硫酸。他澄清说，他和其他人类编辑很少删除链接，除非它们是伪造的。即使有小报或八卦的故事浮出水面，格雷厄姆也不会拒绝他们。他补充说，拥有高业障点的用户倾向于举报这些故事，然后可以将其删除。
 “骇客新闻使我非常难过，”格雷厄姆说。 “我希望社区能够像他们在一个小村庄时那样表现。”
 用户注意到格雷厄姆的无奈。帕塔塞克说，他观察到格雷厄姆非常谨慎，不要告诉别人该说些什么或想什么，但是很显然，他希望人们之间更好地相处，并且随着时间的流逝，他会越来越难过。
  尽管格雷厄姆（Graham）公开表示不想成为下一个Reddit，但很难忽略“黑客新闻”（Hacker News）可能是一个行业的事实。据报道，Reddit以4亿美元的估值筹集现金。尽管Hacker News的流量仅是Reddit的一小部分，但较小的站点实际上在没有任何资金或员工的情况下对企业的估值却令人印象深刻。 
格雷厄姆本人以该网站为主要新闻来源。他甚至通过Hacker News找到了Y Combinator公司。社区中的用户发布了指向Watsi的链接，Watsi是一个非营利性组织，它使急需医疗保健的人们可以为手术和保健筹集资金。他第二次注意到《 Watsi》，并将其发布在《黑客新闻》上，并认为这是一个了不起的主意。他冷嘲热讽创始人，并说服他们成为有史以来由YC支持的第一个非营利组织。格雷厄姆（Graham）最近在沃西（Watsi）担任了第一任董事会主席，这是他有史以来的第一任董事会职位。
 但是格雷厄姆坚称，《黑客新闻》不是企业，也不会成为企业。该网站上没有广告，他没有兴趣通过广告赚钱。他承认，由于他是Y Combinator公司的投资者，因此他通过工作页面间接赚钱，如果公司的员工帮助公司业务，他将不可避免地获利。他也没有兴趣出售该网站。
 很明显，格雷厄姆（Graham）对社区感到沮丧，但当他谈论网站的决定性时刻时，听起来他好像在谈论自己的孩子。他对网站的最鲜明的记忆之一是史蒂夫·乔布斯（Steve Jobs）去世后的第二天，头版上的每个故事都是关于苹果创始人的。
 “用户集体致敬，我发现这是一种显着的方式来展示社区的力量。我以为这确实是一个活生生的东西。就像一群小鸟在天上飞一样，形成了S形。”
 “与Hacker News互动确实有充分的理由，” Ptacek说。 “没有更好的地方可以与黑客社区保持联系……最终，这是一个留言板。重要的是要有一个可以接触到人群并与之交谈的地方。”
 对于Hacker News的未来，很明显，Graham致力于保持质量，并确保社区相互尊重和友善。他说：“我希望大多数Hacker News读者都知道我是在为他们着想。”
note_en:
Hacker News was  initially built by YC co-founder Paul Graham as a demonstration of Arc, a new programming language he’d been working on. He quickly realized that it could help bring together the companies he was supporting and the rest of the folks who wanted in. With 1.6 million page views and 200,000 unique visitors on a given weekday, it’s now a key part of the venture firm’s success.
 But the site quickly took off, as former Redditors flocked to it to talk about tech and startups (the site was then known as Startup News).
  As Graham explains, as the site started seeing traction immediately, he realized this wasn’t just a way to test Arc. He wanted to make Hacker News a place to recreate the way Reddit felt in the good old days, when most of its community was made up of hackers. As Reddit drew more traffic, the hacker focus of the site evolved. The community’s user base became diluted as it grew, and Hacker News was a new home for some of the early Reddit hackers.
  Reddit used to have a good concentration of startup-related links, but that was because so many of Reddit’s initial users were connected in some way to Y Combinator. Now that Reddit is so much more popular, the top links tend to be images, or videos, or political news.
 Another goal of Hacker News, says Graham, was to be a place where founders could share ideas and communicate. In the spirit of Y Combinator’s own incubator, Hacker News was focused on being a community for entrepreneurs and founders in the tech community: a place where they could freely post and where Y Combinator could also get to know potential founders and leaders in the tech world.
 “From the beginning we had a real community, and some of the core group of refugees from Reddit are still prominent on Hacker News today,” Graham explains. Part of what attracted many to Hacker News was its simplicity and voting system. The product’s UI, design and color scheme have remained relatively constant over the past six years.
 Thomas Ptacek, one of the site’s first users, explains that he was a Slashdot user and then a Reddit user, and flocked to Hacker News (at the time Startup News) because it was more relevant to the technology and startup community. He found Hacker News to be a refreshing change from past forums where the quality of commenting was declining.
 Here’s how Hacker News works: Users submit links to stories, and stories are ranked according to a voting system, similar to Reddit. The difference between Hacker News and Reddit, however, is the voting system. While you can vote stories up, you cannot vote stories down (but you can flag stories). According to Graham, 100 upvotes will get a story to the top of the front page of the site. You can only downvote a comment if you have enough “karma” on the site, which is another compelling element of Hacker News. The Karma factor is determined by the number of upvotes on a user’s submission and comments minus the number of downvotes.
 In terms of the design, Graham says he wanted Hacker News to look like your list of processes in a terminal window. The look and feel of the site was aimed at hackers themselves who are familiar with tabular data.
  Graham will occasionally add new features, some of which are on the backend of the site. For example, as comments get more deeply nested and heated in terms of exchange, the reply link takes longer to appear. There is a purposeful drag implemented on this, says Graham, because deeply nested discussions are rarely interesting.
 Another subtle feature addition: a flame-war detector. Graham has been consistently deploying and updating proprietary software that determines whether there is a flame war, where people argue heatedly. When these flame wars take place (which Graham says can often get ugly and personal), the story in which the commenting is taking place is moved further down the page.
 Graham has also created sophisticated spam-detection software, which was just updated with new code six months ago. With the update, Graham says that it’s rare for spam to last on the site for more than 10 minutes. If a user does spam the site or engages in personally vicious behaviors, they run the risk of being banned. But in an interesting twist, called “hellbanning,” the user may not actually know they are banned.
 On the backend, Hacker News runs on one core, and Graham calls this a “remarkable feat of scaling.”
 In terms of human moderation, Graham himself had been spending three to four hours per day simply moderating the site. And that’s in addition to all of his duties running Y Combinator. While a number of other YC alums have moderating abilities, Graham has been the main human element of the site. “It was becoming my life,” he says. Around six months ago, Graham brought on someone else, who he chose not to name, to moderate the site. He says the individual is affiliated with Y Combinator and is a “prudent and thoughtful guy,” and has been doing a great job ever since.
 Hacker News has a strong affiliation with Y Combinator, as well. Graham explains that founders usually all create a Hacker News account when they apply, and that user name is the founder’s identity at Y Combinator. Hacker News also features a jobs page that shows any jobs available at Y Combinator companies. He adds that this jobs portal is very useful for Y Combinator, as the majority of the site’s audience is made up of programmers and engineers.
 If you are a YC founder, your username will show up in orange to other YC founders to enable these entrepreneurs to recognize and meet each other.
 Graham says that Hacker News gets a lot of complaints that it has a bias toward featuring stories about Y Combinator startups, but he says there is no such bias. Instead, the culture at the incubator is to use Hacker News, and with more than 1,000 YC alumni who have graduated from the incubator, many of these founders are still active on the news site and post links to their fellow founders’ launches and news.
 “It was a small intellectual village and now it is a giant city.”
 Growth has its downside. What keeps Graham up at night is worrying about the dilution of quality of the Hacker News. He explains that the site was community of insiders in the hacker world, and it has  gradually been getting diluted. “That is what I spend all my time thinking about,” he says.
 He worries that Hacker News will become what he calls “an old crumbling building.”
 “The community has been in a perpetual but slow decline because the site is growing,” he says.
 Ptacek agrees that the value of Hacker News has changed a bit. “I don’t get a community feel as much, whereas in the beginning it was a small group of people who all know each other,” he says. “It’s less likely now to see the same people from thread to thread.”
 One of Graham’s biggest pain points is the “schoolyard quarrels” he finds on the site on a daily basis, and wishes “users would stop misbehaving.” He cites the example of users organizing voting rings to purposefully vote up stories, which caused Graham to develop additional software to detect this. He adds that more users are trolling under newly created accounts, and are deliberately starting flame wars on the site.
 “I wish I could get people to stop posting comments that are stupid or mean,” he says. “It takes only one or two negative comments and a discussion turns into a flame war.”
 Graham adds that he gets a lot of vitriol from users personally with accusations of bias or censoring. He clarifies that he, and the other human editor, rarely take links down unless they are dupes. Even with tabloid or gossip stories that surface, Graham will not take them down. Users with high karma points tend to flag these stories, he adds, and they can then be taken down.
 “Hacker News makes me sad a lot,” says Graham. “I wish the community would behave the way they did when it was a little village.”
 Users are noticing Graham’s frustrations. Ptacek says that he observes that Graham is careful not to tell people what to say or think, but it’s clear that he wants people to treat each other better and he gets more sad over time.
  While Graham is open about not wanting to be the next Reddit, it’s hard to ignore the fact that Hacker News could be a business. Reddit is reportedly  raising cash at a $400 million valuation. While Hacker News has a fraction of the traffic that Reddit does, the smaller site could actually have an impressive valuation as a business without any funding or employees.
 Graham himself uses the site as his primary source of news. He’s even found Y Combinator companies through Hacker News. A user in the community posted a link to Watsi, a non-profit that allows people in dire need of medical care to raise money for procedures and health care. He noticed Watsi the second time it was posted on Hacker News and thought it was an amazing idea. He cold-called the founders and convinced them to be the first ever YC-backed nonprofit. And Graham recently took a first board seat at Watsi,  his first board position ever.
 But Graham is adamant that Hacker News is not a business and would not become a business. There are no ads on the site, and he has no interest in making money from ads. He admits that through the jobs page he indirectly makes money, as he is an investor in Y Combinator companies and will inevitably profit if the company’s hires help the business. Nor would he be interested in selling the site.
 While it’s clear that Graham has his frustrations with the community, when he talks about the site’s defining moments, he sounds like he is speaking about his own child. One of his most distinct memories about the site is the day following Steve Jobs’ death, when every story on the front page was about the Apple founder.
 “Users did it collectively as a tribute, and I found this a really remarkable way to show the power of a community. I thought this is really a living, breathing thing. It was like a bunch of birds flying through the sky forming themselves as an S.”
 “There are really good reasons to engage with Hacker News,” says Ptacek. “There is no better place to stay engaged with the hacker community…At the end of day it is a message board. Having a place where you can reach and talk to groups of people is an important concept.”
 As for the future of Hacker News, it’s clear that Graham is focused on maintaining quality and making sure that the community treats each other with respect and kindness. “I hope that most Hacker News readers know that I am doing this for their sake,” he says.
title: 黑客新闻的演变（2013）

根据周一向美国证券交易委员会提交的10-K备案文件，位于加州帕洛阿尔托的特斯拉公司（Tesla Inc.）在2020年的401（k）计划中未做出任何匹配的贡献。
 该文件显示，这是该汽车制造商连续第三年未对其401（k）计划做出任何贡献。
 Tesla Inc. 401（k）计划会自动以5％的员工延期率招募计划参与者。根据该计划最新的5500表文件，您可以选择缴纳更多的薪金。
 根据5500表格，该计划允许在满足某些资格要求的前提下，对雇主酌情进行匹配和/或对雇主进行非自愿性非自愿捐款。根据5500表格，这些要求在2019计划年度未得到满足。
 特斯拉为401（k）计划做出贡献的那些要求的性质不可用。截至12月31日，该汽车制造商的股价上涨了695％。
 根据特斯拉公司最新的5500表文件，截至2019年12月31日，特斯拉公司的401（k）计划拥有6.33亿美元的资产。
note_en:
Tesla Inc., Palo Alto, Calif., did not make any matching contributions to its 401(k) plan in 2020, according to its 10-K filing with the SEC on Monday.
 It is the third straight year the automaker has not made any contributions to its 401(k) plan, according to the filing.
 The Tesla Inc. 401(k) Plan automatically enrolls participants in the plan with a deferral rate of 5% of employees' salaries with the option to contribute more, according to the plan's most recent Form 5500 filing.
 According to the Form 5500, the plan allows for an "employer discretionary matching and/or an employer discretionary non-elective contribution subject to certain eligibility requirements." Those requirements were not met for the 2019 plan year, according to the Form 5500.
 The nature of those requirements for Tesla to make contributions to the 401(k) plan was not available. The stock price for the automaker was up 695% for the year ended Dec. 31.
 As of Dec. 31, 2019, the Tesla Inc. 401(k) Plan had $633 million in assets, according to the company's most recent Form 5500 filing.
title: 特斯拉连续第三年跳过401（k）比赛
title_en: Tesla skips 401(k) match for third straight year

 信息专员办公室（ICO）表示，Facebook允许严重违规行为。法律。
  10月，由米尔伯格伦敦律师事务所代表的Facebook You Owe Us组对Facebook提起了类似的大规模法律诉讼。
  由于这种情况相似之处，高等法院可以决定将两者合并或同时保留。 
尽管在英国没有采取大规模法律行动的先例，但在美国却没有。  Google同意在2012年美国联邦贸易委员会（FTC）提起的同一宗案件中，支付创纪录的2250万美元（合1680万英镑）。
note_en:
Facebook is being sued for "losing control" of the data of about a million users in England and Wales.
 The alleged failings were revealed in the Cambridge Analytica scandal, where harvested data was used for advertising during elections.
  Facebook told BBC News there was “no evidence" UK or EU users’ data had been transferred to Cambridge Analytica.
 But the case against the technology giant, expected to last for at least three years, will argue a “loss of control” over users' personal data warrants individual compensation.
 The harvesting of Facebook users' personal information by third-party apps was at the centre of the Cambridge Analytica privacy scandal, exposed in 2018.
  Cambridge Analytica's app on Facebook had harvested the data of people who interacted with it - and that of friends who had not given consent.
  And the case is being represented by law firm Hausfield “on behalf of those Facebook friends”.
   The action seeks damages from Facebook over failure to comply with the Data Protection Act 1998.
 A Facebook spokesman said: "The Information Commissioner's Office investigation into these issues, which included seizing and interrogating Cambridge Analytica's servers, found no evidence that any UK or EU users' data was transferred by [app developer] Dr [Aleksandr] Kogan to Cambridge Analytica."
 But Mr Jukes told BBC News it was not about “where the data went” but rather “that Facebook didn’t care".
    In October 2018, the UK's data-protection watchdog fined Facebook its maximum penalty of £500,000 for its role in the Cambridge Analytica scandal.
 The Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) said Facebook had allowed a "serious breach" of the law.
  A similar mass legal action was filed against Facebook in October, by the group Facebook You Owe Us, represented by law firm Milberg London.
  Because of the cases' similarities, the High Court may decide to merge the two or hold them simultaneously.
  Although there is no precedent for such a mass legal action in the UK, there is in the US.
 Google agreed to pay a record $22.5m (£16.8m) in a case brought by the US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) on the same issue in 2012.
title: Facebook起诉“失去对用户数据的控制”
title_en: Facebook sued for 'losing control' of users’ data

PWA功能强大，有效，快速且类似于应用程序。很难想象通过PWA实施无法显着改善的移动网络媒体资源。他们还可以潜在地消除对当今存在的许多虚荣本机应用程序的需求。渐进式Web应用程序适合所有人，无论是台式机用户还是移动用户。我认为必须重申没有移动网络很重要。
   渐进式Web应用程序已从本机和Web应用程序中获取了功能。无论移动操作系统和设备类型如何，它们都可以快速运行，也可以提供丰富的功能，就像它们是为特定设备开发的一样。由于易于安装，可发现性，自动更新，缓慢的网络甚至是脱机工作模式，PWA可以增强用户体验。
 PWA不需要为各种设备使用不同的版本；单个渐进式应用程序可以满足其运行的所有端点的要求。因此，它大大减少了开发人员提供的工作量，因此，降低了创建PWA的成本。成本比本地移动应用程序低三到四倍。
 如今，移动用户更喜欢应用程序而不是浏览器，因为应用程序比浏览器更受青睐，因为它们更加用户友好，可以脱机运行并且具有更吸引人的界面。渐进式Web应用程序通过结合移动应用程序的外观和最佳网站性能来提供高级用户体验。
  与常规的移动应用程序不同，PWA不需要漫长而复杂的安装过程，从而极大地改善了用户体验。用户只需将应用程序直接直接下载到其设备即可，而无需访问App Store或Google Play。它简化了流程，并大大减少了用户的放弃。
  从iOS 11.3开始，可以在Apple设备上运行PWA，但是您可以忘记与旧设备的兼容性。
 PWA出现已经有几年了，因此带有过时的Web浏览器的较旧的移动设备对它们的支持不太好也就不足为奇了。虽然这个问题将来不可避免地会解决 
它们的性能也不如本地应用程序的性能好，这与JavaScript是一种单线程编程语言有关。 目前，仍然缺少对某些重要设备功能的访问，包括蓝牙，接近传感器，环境光，高级摄像头控件等。  渐进式Web应用程序（PWA）是一种Web应用程序，其开发的功能使其具有浏览时的体验。 PWA可以在一定程度上改善用户体验，使用户感觉与使用本机应用程序时的感觉相同。
note_en:
PWAs are powerful, effective, fast and app-like. It's hard to imagine a mobile web property that could not be significantly improved via PWA implementation. They can also potentially eliminate the need for many vanity native apps that exist today. progressive web apps are for everyone, desktop and mobile users alike. I think it’s important to reiterate that there is no mobile web.
   Progressive web apps have taken functionality from both native and web apps. They can run fast regardless of mobile operating systems and device types also providing rich functionality as if they were developed for a specific device. Thanks to easy installation, discoverability, automated updates, slow network or even the offline work mode, PWAs can enhance user experience.
 PWAs do not require different versions for various devices; a single progressive app meets the requirements of all endpoints on which it operates. So, it significantly reduces the amount of efforts that developers provide, and as a result, the cost to create a PWA decreases. The cost is three or four times lower than that of a native mobile app.
 Nowadays, mobile users prefer apps to browsers as applications compare favorably with browsers, as they are more user-friendly, can operate offline and have a more attractive interface. Progressive web applications provide an advanced user experience by combining the look and feel of mobile applications and the best of website performance.
  Unlike regular mobile applications, PWAs do not require a long and complex installation process, which significantly improves user experience. Users just download an app, quickly and directly to their devices, and they do not need to go to the App Store or Google Play. It streamlines the procedure and significantly reduces user abandonment.
  Since iOS 11.3, it’s been possible to run PWAs on Apple devices, but you can forget about compatibility with older devices.
 PWAs have been around for just a few years, so it shouldn’t come as a surprise that older mobile devices with outdated web browsers don’t support them too well. While this problem will inevitably solve itself in the future
 Their performance is also not as good as the performance of native apps, which has a lot to do with the fact that JavaScript is a single-threaded programming language. At the moment, access to certain important device features is still missing, including Bluetooth, proximity sensors, ambient light, advanced camera controls, and others.
  Progressive Web App (PWA) is a web app, developed with features which give it an app like experience while browsing. PWA improves the user experience to the extent that users can feel the same as they do while using native apps.
title: 为什么PWA是未来

note_en:
GamersNexus has been a staple of our RSS feeds for more than a decade. The site has quickly become a must read for anyone looking to build a PC, especially a  gaming PC. And in addition to running that enterprise, Editor in Chief Steve Burke has more recently become a staple of our weekly viewing, too, as he helms  GamersNexus' equally popular YouTube channel.
  It doesn't take a lot of time to realize GamersNexus clearly shares a lot of DNA with the Orbital HQ. In every video, Burke and his team both inform and entertain, skimping neither technical jargon nor opportunities  to create useful reddit memes. By now, GamersNexus videos have focused on everything from  putting PCs from Walmart through genuine technical paces to emptying ( literally,  emptying) a tube of thermal paste on a poor CPU. You'll learn useful info every time, even if it's  what new parts  not to covet.     "I think we made it pretty clear in the video, but if someone is trying to build a PC and wonders, 'Hmm, how do I apply thermal paste?" they type in 'thermal paste,' they click on this video, and then they watch five seconds and go, 'Oh, I get it. I don't need the rest of this tutorial,"' Burke told Ars in our recent Personal History interview. "Otherwise they'd have a pretty messy CPU socket, I think." (Burke, by the way, suggests the best way to actually apply thermal paste is just by making a quick X. "It allows me to get CPUs up to 5GhZ, 6GhZ, no problem.") In our 25 minute chat above, Burke is generous with the liquid nitrogen, shares tips for making successful how-to videos, and fields troubleshooting questions on everything from all black wiring to handling dead bugs in the power supply. Come for the discussion of CPU fans, stay for learning why component jargon is the one true universal language.

DENVER (CBS4) – The internet has expanded kids’ access to information. Now, two state lawmakers want to help them identify misinformation and disinformation.
 From Facebook and Twitter to blogs and apps, the information landscape is vast and State Rep. Barbara McLachlan said facts can vary widely.
 RELATED:  Denver City Council Passes Controversial Group Living Amendment  “Whenever somebody starts off a sentence with ‘I read somewhere that this happened’, you think, wait a minute. I think we all need to be a little distrustful about where people are getting their information,” McLachlan said.
 A school teacher turned state lawmaker, McLachlan says kids who consume news almost exclusively online are especially vulnerable. A study by Stanford University found 82 percent of middle school students can’t tell the difference between an online ad and a news story.
 McLachlan and State Rep. Lisa Cutter are carrying a bill to improve media literacy among kids.
 “We don’t want to teach kids what to think,” says Cutter. “We’re not taking a stand that one side is right and another is wrong, or one outlet is good and one outlet is bad. We just want to give them the tools so they can figure it out and understand what makes a credible source and then they can form their own opinions about that information.”
 RELATED:  Animal Rights Lawyer Jennifer Emmi Jailed in 'Murder For Hire' Plot The bill creates an online bank of media literacy resources in the Department of Education to help teachers incorporate media literacy in everyday curriculum.
 “So it’s not like everybody stops and says okay now we’re going to do a unit on media literacy,” says McLachlan, “It’s every time you do a research paper, every time you’re having a discussion in class and you’re referring to an article or something on TV, that’s’ where media literacy will be taught.”
  Cutter passed a law two years ago to establish a task force to study media literacy. The online bank of resources was compiled based on its recommendations.
  MORE:  Amid Short Supply, COVID-19 Vaccines Are Being Bought & Sold Online “There’s outside entities that are trying to sow distrust in public health, in vaccines. They’ve traced some of that to China, Russia with election meddling. So I absolutely, firmly believe this will lead to a healthier democracy and society,” she said.

FirstMark是现有的投资者，对nextmv的投资增加了一倍，并领导了A轮融资。其他投资者包括Github CTO Jason Warner，Seamless创始人Jason Finger，Stripe COO Claire Johnson，Greenhawk Capital的Ankit Agarwal以及其他机构投资者，例如2048，发电机，和非典型。
   nextmv由Carolyn Mooney和Ryan O'Neil创立，简化了优化和测试以物流为中心的决策模型的过程。考虑一下像GrubHub这样的公司，二人曾在此工作。每次在平台上收到订单时，GrubHub都要权衡各种优先事项，从交付速度到驾驶员的行驶里程再到整体效率。
 根据公司的关键绩效指标或KPI来优化运行这些平台的算法需要大量的资源和人才。而且，如果这些KPI发生更改，或者需要对算法进行调整，则需要更多的资源来测试这些修改。
 而且，您需要一个模拟环境来测试更改，这是nextmv自启动以来提供的功能。
 在此最新一轮之后，nextmv正在努力简化其产品。该公司已经启动了nextmv Cloud，以允许开发人员（不仅仅是运营研究人员）使用其软件。 nextmv Cloud在路由和交付空间中具有快速启动模型，可以使开发人员实现决策自动化，并计划在未来几个月内将其引入新的垂直领域。
 在nextmv之前，可能需要整个运营研究人员团队来优化决策模型和模拟测试环境。当nextmv推出时，一个团队仍需要至少一名运营研究人员才能使用该产品。现在，开发人员可以跳上平台并开始使用。
  Mooney说：“像我的联合创始人一样，有成千上万的运营研究人员试图以研究能力或公司内部非常专业的团队来解决这些问题。” “但是那里有数百万开发人员。为什么这不是每个开发人员都拥有的超级大国。 Twilio做到了这一点，因此每个工程师都可以成为消息传递工程师。” 
很大一部分资金将用于建立Cloud平台，以生产和分发nextmv技术并提高可访问性，但该公司还利用资金来增加内容部门，以帮助用户浏览软件。  最终，nextmv希望建立平台的社区分支，让开发人员彼此展示他们如何自动化决策，这与Figma社区不同。
note_en:
FirstMark, an existing investor, doubled down on nextmv and led the Series A. Other investors include Github CTO Jason Warner, Seamless founder Jason Finger, Stripe COO Claire Johnson, Ankit Agarwal from Greenhawk Capital, as well as other institutional investors such as  2048,  Dynamo, and  Atypical.
   Founded by Carolyn Mooney and Ryan O’Neil, nextmv simplifies the process of optimizing and testing logistics-focused decision models. Consider a company like GrubHub, where the duo worked previously. GrubHub has to weigh a wide variety of priorities each time an order comes through on the platform, from speed of delivery to mileage on the drivers’ cars to overall efficiency.
 It takes an immense amount of resources, and personnel talent, to optimize the algorithm running those platforms based on the company’s key performance indicators, or KPIs. And if those KPIs change, or tweaks to the algorithm need to be made, it takes even more resources to test those modifications.
 And you would need a simulated environment to test the changes, something that nextmv has provided since its launch.
 On the heels of this latest round, nextmv is working to simplify its product. The company has launched nextmv Cloud to allow developers, not just operations researchers, to use its software. nextmv Cloud has quick start models in the routing and delivery space to allow developers to automate decision-making, with plans to launch into new verticals in the coming months.
 Before nextmv, it could take an entire team of operations researchers to optimize decision-making models and simulate test environments. When nextmv launched, a team still needed to have at least one operations researcher to use the product. Now, developers can hop on the platform and get started.
  “There are hundreds of thousands of operations researchers, like my cofounder, out there that are trying to solve these problems in a research capacity or in very specialized groups within companies,” said Mooney. “But there are millions of developers out there. Why can’t this be a superpower that every developer has in their pocket. It’s the same way that Twilio made it so that every every engineer could be a messaging engineer.”
  A good chunk of the funding is going toward building out that Cloud platform to productize and distribute the nextmv technology and increase accessibility, but the company is also using funding to ramp up a content arm to help users explore the software.
 Eventually, nextmv wants to build out a community arm of the platform, letting developers show each other how they’re automating decisions, not unlike Figma Community.

 Eniac的基金规模在过去十年中急剧增长，从2010年的160万美元第一只基金到2017年的1亿美元第四只基金。但是，Mehta表示，研究小组以1.25亿美元作为最新目标，同时也是“硬顶。”
  更大的资金使Eniac可以进行更多投资，并随着种子交易的定义扩展而进行领导。 （该公司表示，它可以在单个回合中投资35万至300万美元。）同时，GP Hadley Harris强调，Eniac将继续专注于种子交易，而不是As系列，并且希望保持“真正的合作” ，这样我们就不需要进行超过一轮的比赛了。”
 Eniac的普通合伙人通常每年仅进行两到三项投资，哈里斯说，这“比普通种子基金要少得多”。
  他继续说道：“我们一直希望成为我们投资，领导或共同领导这些轮次的公司的记录投资者。” “而且我们希望在他们的旅程的早期阶段就拥有与他们成为合作伙伴的带宽。我们认为做到这一点的唯一方法就是专心。”
 Eniac的投资组合现在包括120多家公司，并拥有50多个出口。最近的成功包括移动消息公司Attentive（去年秋天筹集了2.3亿美元的D系列），播客创业公司Anchor（被Spotify收购）和在线零售商Boxed（最近与亚洲最大的实体公司之一Aeon合作）。
 Eniac最初自称为一家专注于移动设备的公司，但现在投资于软件即服务，开发人员平台，消费者和深度技术领域。哈里斯说，他们没有针对他们关注的任何特定行业或趋势，因为，“我们希望在论文驱动和机会主义之间取得平衡……我们倾向于关注的问题往往是针对每个合作伙伴的。并迅速改变。” 
该公司总部位于纽约，在旧金山设有办事处。哈德利说，纽约的初创公司仍然是当务之急，与此同时，“我们也相信，伟大的创始人可以在任何地方，我们对分布式团队越来越感兴趣。”
  辛格补充说，尽管对其他新兴创业中心进行了大肆宣传，但“我们在纽约与之合作的许多创始人仍留在纽约。他们还没有离开。”
 他说，他们可能会在其他地方雇用团队成员，但是远程员工的门槛很高。如果一家初创公司想要完全分布式，“首先必须完全远程并分布式。您以后不能再分发了；您必须从一开始就非常有意地以这种方式建立组织。”
 至于团队的寿命，我注意到从多元化的角度来看，这也有潜在的不利影响，特别是因为所有四个创始GP都是男性。但是，该公司最近又提拔了另外两个团队成员-财务与运营副总裁Anna Nitschke和投资者Kristin McDonald-辛格指出``对我们的交易进行投票''并且``似乎对她如何在公司中拥有同等的发言权感到满意。跑。”该公司计划今年雇用6名新的团队成员。
 如果您想了解公司合作伙伴的更多信息，我将在2月10日美国东部时间下午2:30在Superpeer（另一项Eniac最新投资）上采访他们。
note_en:
Eniac’s four general partners — Hadley Harris, Nihal Mehta, Vic Singh and Tim Young — have been making seed investments together for more than a decade, and they’ve known each other for even longer, having first met at the University of Pennsylvania. Singh described the firm as “one of the OGs” in seed investing, while Mehta said, “Consistency has been our superpower.”
 The size of Eniac’s funds has grown dramatically over the past decade, from  its $1.6 million first fund in 2010 to  its $100 million fourth fund in 2017. However, Mehta said the team approached the latest fund with $125 million as both a goal and a “hard cap.”
  The larger funds allow Eniac to make more investments, and to lead rounds as the definition of a seed deal has expanded. ( The firm says it can invest anywhere from $350,000 to $3 million in a single round.) At the same time, GP Hadley Harris emphasized that Eniac will remain focused on seed deals rather than Series As, and that it wants to remain “really collaborative, so that we never need to take more than half the round.”
 Eniac’s general partners usually only two or three investments each per year, which Harris said is “quite a bit less than average seed fund.”
  “We always want to be the investor of record in the companies that we invest in, leading or co-leading these rounds,” he continued. “And we want to have the bandwidth to be partners with them in the early stages of their journey. We think the only way to do that is concentration.”
 Eniac’s  portfolio now includes more than 120 companies, with 50-plus exits. Recent successes include mobile messaging company Attentive (which  raised a $230 million Series D last fall), podcasting startup Anchor ( acquired by Spotify) and online retailer Boxed (which  recently partnered with one of Asia’s largest brick-and-mortar companies, Aeon).
 While Eniac initially billed itself as a mobile-focused firm, it now invests across software-as-a-service, developer platforms, consumer and deeptech. Harris said they’re not pointing to any specific industries or trends that they’re focused on because, “We want to be balanced between thesis-driven and opportunistic … The theses that we tend to focus on tend to be on a per-partner basis and change pretty quickly.”
 The firm is headquartered in New York, with an office in San Francisco. Hadley said New York-based startups remain a priority, while at the same time, “We also believe that great founders can be anywhere and we’re more and more interested in distributed teams.”
  Singh added that despite the hype around other emerging startup hubs, “A lot of the founders we partner with in New York are staying in New York. They have not left.”
 They might hire team members elsewhere, he said, but there’s a high bar for remote employees. And if a startup wants to be fully distributed, “You have to be fully remote and distributed first. You can’t go distributed later; you have to very intentionally build the organization in that way from the start.”
 As for the team’s longevity, I noted that it also has a potential downside from a diversity perspective, especially since all four of the founding GPs are men. However, the firm has recently promoted two other team members — Vice President of Finance and Operations Anna Nitschke and Investor Kristin McDonald — who Singh noted “votes on deals with us” and “feels as if she has an equal say in how the firm is run.” And the firm plans to hire  six new team members this year.
 If you want to hear more from the firm’s partners, I’ll be  interviewing them on Superpeer (another  recent Eniac investment) tomorrow, February 10, at 2:30pm Eastern.
